responses.question: In a typical week, what is the total number of hours you work across all your roles combined?
How many hours per week do you spend face-to-face (or on calls) with customers — across presales and delivery?
What are the main activities that make up your customer-facing time?
responses.answer:
Pre-Sales Related Discussions like understanding customer requirements or their current system landscape.
When assigned in a project, effort goes upto 32 hours a week in the customer project itself. Main activities inclulde leading the architecture and ownership of the implementation.

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Other / Initiative responsibilities?
In your Other / Initiative role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer:
1. Leading, Planning and Execution of Migration Hub across Strategy, Architecture, Operations.  
2. Leading Architect Practice within STP, Pre-Sales, architect enablement, Academy, CTA Preparation (Self)
3. Taking Interviews for Architects and Directors, Deep Tech
4. Self Skill up / Certifications

responses.question: Still in your Other / Initiative role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer: I think all the activities currently taken up are of high value.

responses.question: What is the single biggest thing that prevents you from spending time on what matters most in your role?
responses.answer:
Taking Interviews - In the past I have been doing interviews from multiple practices/clouds. This has improved since July where I am focussing on STP related hiring mostly.

Second would be Adhoc operational meetings/tasks

responses.question: If you could redesign your week, what would you spend significantly more time on — and what would you hand off or stop doing?
responses.answer:
I want to focus heavily on the Migration Hub right now. 

I’d like to pause new customer project engagements untill Migration Hub starts delivering value. Getting into a customer project requires heavy focus and time and is very difficult to balance along.
